title: Sliding Window Maximum
prompt:
You are given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `k` representing the size of a sliding window which moves from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the `k` numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position.

Return a list of the maximum value in each sliding window.

starterCode.python:
def solve(nums: list[int], k: int) -> list[int]:
    pass
starterCode.typescript:
export function solve(nums: number[], k: number): number[] {
  
}

notesForAI.coreIdea: Use a monotonic decreasing deque. The deque stores indices. For each new element, remove all indices from the back whose values are less than the current element. Remove front index if it's outside the window. The front of the deque is always the index of the maximum in the current window.
commonBugs: Not removing expired indices from the front of the deque
Storing values instead of indices in the deque (makes expiry checks impossible)
Off-by-one when determining when to start recording results (first result at index k-1)
